title: quercus robur f. heterophylla
intro: Quercus robur f. heterophylla, also known as the English oak, is a subspecies of the pedunculate oak tree, characterized by its distinctive leaf variation. The medicinal properties of Quercus robur f. heterophylla include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and astringent properties, which contribute to its use in traditional herbal medicine. The health benefits of this plant include the potential to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health, as well as its use as an antimicrobial agent to support immune function. Quercus robur f. heterophylla is typically prepared as a tincture, tea, or infusion, and may also be found in herbal supplements and topical creams, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects on human health.
